texto: Remember that our Heavenly Father knows us perfectly and knows what's best for us . . . . Surely, His knowledge is greater than ours. We just have to have faith in Him. If we don't, well, that doesn't change what happened, it just makes us more miserable because we refuse to trust His will. Our Heavenly Father wants us to have joy. And happiness. But we need to look for those opportunities that give us joy. If we don't, what would be the purpose for existing?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jeri Gilchrist que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Remember that our Heavenly Father knows us perfectly and knows what's best for us . . . . Surely,…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, faith, god, com foco em mudanca e god.
